title: Mantaris-class amphibious medium transport

{{Quote|That's a Mantaris Amphibious Transport. The Gungans are using one of those to colonize the water moon of Naboo.|Padmé Amidala|<ref name="YC">''[[Star Wars: Yoda's Challenge Activity Center]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Mantaris''-class amphibious medium transport''' was a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] designed and constructed jointly by [[Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps/Legends|Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps]] and [[Otoh Gunga Bongameken Cooperative/Legends|Otoh Gunga Bongowerks]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Because of its unique design based on a [[manta ray]], that [[Transport/Legends|transport]] was also referred to as the '''Ray Ship''', or simply '''Ray'''. It was developed in the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] that followed the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], as part of a cooperative effort between the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] and the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] to [[Colonization of Ohma-D'un/Legends|colonize]] the empty [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]]. Due to its conception, the ''Mantaris''-class was remarkably adaptable to different environments, which included [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] flight, [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]], and underwater. Ultimately, the commissioning of the ''Mantaris''-class transport helped the Gungans join the [[galactic community]] at large. [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala's]] [[Master of Sciences/Legends|Master of Sciences]] [[Graf Zapalo/Legends|Graf Zapalo]], who had supervised the conception of the ''Mantaris''-class, also adopted it as his personal means of transport.

==Characteristics==
===Physical appearance===
[[File:Mantarayship.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Wireframe schematics of a ''Mantaris''-class transport]]
{{Quote|Go now, get on the big bongo!|Boss Nass|<ref name="TGF" />}}
The ''Mantaris''-class amphibious medium [[Transport/Legends|transport]]<ref name="WoTC" />—also known as "the Ray Ship" or simply "the Ray"<ref name="YC" />—was a 98-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] designed for travel both in [[Realspace/Legends|interstellar]] and in [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] environments. Being a joint venture between the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] and the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]], its design reflected the artistic qualities of both races.<ref name="WoTC" /> The class was named after the [[manta ray]], a fish species it resembled very much, with its flat [[Wing (technology)|wings]] swept backwards and its two saber-like tails.<ref name="TGF" /> Those tails were in fact heat sink finials that measured 30 meters, a little less than a third of the ship's total length.<ref name="WoTC" /> When activated, phosphorescent knobs located at the tips of the wings shone [[Color/Legends|red]]. It also featured horn-shaped, finlike appendages located the bow,<ref name="TGF" /> which were twin [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launchers. The overall frame of the ''Mantaris'' transport was based on the Gungan cargo submersible, better known as [[bongo]],<ref name="WoTC" /> which is why it was often referred to in [[Gungan (language)|Gungan Basic]] as "big bongo ship."<ref name="TGF" /> That well-tested hull design, which was only adapated to water, was then enhanced by Naboo engineers to make it spaceworthy,<ref name="WoTC" /> and capable of withstanding important pressure changes. It was also equipped with [[electromotive stabilizer]]s for softer landings.<ref name="YC" />

===Interior layout===
[[File:Mantaris_cockpit.png|thumb|The cockpit of a ''Mantaris''-class transport]]
The normal cargo capacity of the ''Mantaris''-class was of 250 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]], an improvement compared with earlier bongos. In its default configuration, the vessel could carry twenty passengers, [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]] for three [[Standard month/Legends|months]], and a crew of four—one [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], one [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]]/shield operator, one [[Communications officer/Legends|communications]]/security officer, one engineer/[[Gunner/Legends|gunner]], and one [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]]. In addition to that, the transport could be outfitted with bubble-shaped modules that could either add thirty individuals to the passenger capacity, increase the cargo capacity by 100 tons, or carry wildlife specimens<ref name="WoTC" /> contained in [[liqui-stasis tank]].<ref name="YC" /> Every ''Mantaris''-class transport could carry up to four modules at a time. In turn, a single module pod could contain sixty small creatures, thirty medium ones, fifteen large ones, seven huge ones, three gargantuan ones, or a single colossal creature. It could also carry [[ER-1 probe droid]]s.<ref name="WoTC" /> In its cockpit, the vessel also boasted a large [[holoviewer]] that projected [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] images constructed from the probe droids' sensors array. The main dashboard was equipped with various tools that allowed the operator to perform several tasks on ground while staying aboard, through control of the probe droid. Those tools included the species indicator, which could select a given organism and monitor it from the cockpit. The capture tool would prompt the droid to return a given organism back to the ship's tanks, while the release tool did the opposite. Finally, the stun tool caused the droid to temporarily immobilize the selected creature with a stun beam.<ref name="TGF" />

==Role==
{{Quote|Wanna yousa bring da plants and animals in the big bongo ship?|Boss Nass, to Amidala and Kenobi|<ref name="TGF" />}}
The ''Manta''-class transport was intended for transporting various organisms, whether they be [[Plant/Legends|plants]] or animals, from one celestial body to another.<ref name="TGF" /> It eventually became a more common type of transport for passengers,<ref name="WoTC" /> and some individuals used it as a private vessel.<ref name="FF104">{{FactFile|104|ZAP 1-2|Graf Zapalo (German Edition)}}</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|All the lifeforms wesa collected no fit on the big bongo ship. Choose mighty careful, they must be balanced.|Boss Nass|<ref name="TGF" />}}
The design of the ''Mantaris''-class amphibious medium transport came to fruition in the months that followed the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]<ref name="WoTC" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The schematics of an early version of it, titled "Mantaris Amphibious Transport," were already present in the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] files of the [[J-type 327 Nubian royal starship/Legends|Naboo royal starship]] very shortly after the end of the invasion.<ref name="YC" /> It was specifically designed when the Naboo and Gungan peoples put a cooperative effort to [[Colonization of Ohma-D'un/Legends|colonize]] [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]], the primary [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="WoTC" /> The project, which was spearheaded by the Gungan leader [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] and endorsed by [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], required the creation of a number of new technologies, since the Gungans had not yet entered [[Technology level|space age]] by then. To create those new tools, [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] from [[Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps/Legends|Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps]] and from [[Otoh Gunga Bongameken Cooperative/Legends|Otoh Gunga Bongowerks]] worked under the guidance of [[Graf Zapalo/Legends|Graf Zapalo]], [[Master of Sciences/Legends|Master of Sciences]] to Queen Amidala. The success of the ''Mantaris''-class program ultimately marked the introduction of the Gungans into the larger [[galactic community]]. Following the success of the colonization mission, the amphibious transport became the Gungans' principle means of transport between worlds,<ref name="db">{{DB|character|bossnass|Nass, Boss}}</ref> and Graf Zapalo himself adopted one as his personal vehicle.<ref name="FF104" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ray_ship.png|thumb|250px|The original stats for the ''Mantaris''-class, as shown in ''Yoda's Challenge'']]

===Creation===
The ''Mantaris''-class transport was created for ''[[Star Wars Episode I: The Gungan Frontier]]'',<ref name="TGF" /> an educational ecological simulation developed by [[Lucas Learning]] for simultaneous release with [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], the first installment of the [[prequel trilogy]].<ref name="Daggert">{{WebCite|url=http://www.teraskasi.net/sperry_051706_01.htm|text=Star Wars: The Gungan Frontier|work=DaggertWeb|archivedate=20080211224053}}</ref>

===Canon discrepancies===
In the [[August]] 1999, the ''Mantaris''-class transport appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Yoda's Challenge Activity Center]]'' another Lucas Learning video game. The tangram mini-game of ''Yoda's Challenge'' included several data sheets of characters and vehicles as rewards. One of those sheets was devoted to the ''Mantaris''-class transport, giving it the nickname "Ray Ship" and providing technical stats with a brief history.<ref name="YC" /> However, on [[February 17]], [[2001]], those early stats were disregarded when [[Steve Miller]] wrote "The ''Mantaris''", an online supplement to the [[Wizards of the Coast]] paperback ''[[Secrets of Naboo]]''.<ref name="WoTC" /> The data sheet from ''Yoda's Challenge'' and Miller's stats differ on a great number of points, from the ship's length to its manufacturer or hyperdrive capability.<ref name="WoTC" /><ref name="YC" />
